# Phase 1 Ideation SBDC Resources

> BOSS Libraries public routing record. Use it to find the right source, link, organization, or person.

## Description

## Lifecycle-stage routing summary Use Start Your Business, Business Advising, startup webinars/classes, and the local SBDC locator. Ask about concept, customer, problem, geography, experience, and next decision before advising formation. ## BOSS next best action

## When to surface

Surface when the user's question concerns formation, funding, legal and compliance during Ideation, Formation, Launch and this source is relevant to the next document or human handoff.

## Key information

- ## Lifecycle-stage routing summary
- Use Start Your Business, Business Advising, startup webinars/classes, and the local SBDC locator. Ask about concept, customer, problem, geography, experience, and next decision before advising formation.
- ## BOSS next best action
- Ask one clarifying question that identifies the user's immediate decision, then recommend the specific SBDC resource and local-office path.
- ## Guardrail
- Do not jump ahead to formation, funding, contracting, or scaling before the stage-specific prerequisites are clear.

## People and links

- Organizations: Small Business Development Center (SBDC)
- Link: <https://www.sbdc.uh.edu/sbdc/Start_Your_Business.asp>

## Next handoff

Open the cited source link when available and route follow-up to Small Business Development Center (SBDC) or the named qualified resource.

## Guardrails

- The BOSS Libraries Librarian retrieves and routes information; it is not a mentor or a substitute for a qualified professional.
- Do not make final legal, tax, accounting, lending, certification, procurement, or compliance decisions for the user.
- Verify live pages for current eligibility, deadlines, contacts, prices, and program rules before presenting them as current.
